text:
Limitation × Vow × Chain of Judgment (制約×誓約×戒めの鎖, Seiyaku × Seiyaku × Imashime no Kusari) is the 45 th episode of the original Hunter × Hunter anime. It first aired on November 11 th , 2000.

Overview

Kurapika's flashbacks tell how he passed his hidden Hunter exam and got his chains, what Nen type he has, and how he's changed. He gets his first job and gets on a train where he meets someone that may be a future ally.

Synopsis

Kurapika, while trying to develop his Nen, seeks something that will help him capture the Phantom Troupe and get retribution. During this process, chains emerge from Kurapika's palm and entangle his body.

Sometime later, Kurapika boards a train while reminiscing on when he first tried to get Hunter jobs and how little he knew about Nen back then. 

In a flashback, Kurapika's Master has him perform the Water Divination test and determines that Kurapika is a Conjurer. Kurapika is disappointed by this result, having hoped to be an Enhancer. Kurapika's Master says there is nothing Kurapika can do about this and has him train his Hatsu for the next month. After this month is up, Kurapika's Master finds Kurapika's training successful and tells him he has officially passed the Secret Hunter Exam. However, this only angers Kurapika, who insists he needs more strength and tries to get his Master to teach him Enhancer abilities.

Kurapika's Master has him sit down and explain the compatibility between Nen types to him, trying to convince Kurapika that learning Enhancer techniques may put him at a disadvantage in certain circumstances. Instead, Kurapika's Master tries to get Kurapika to consider his conjuration abilities and what might be possible for him to conjure. The two always fight over Kurapika's ability, and Kurapika walks away from his Master.

In the present, Melody enters Kurapika's train car, and he helps her with her luggage. Melody tries to make an amicable conversation with Kurapika, but he is completely uninterested and only wants to be alone. A ticket verifier comes by and checks Kurapika's ticket and ID, and Melody notices the man's heart rate increase when he looks over Kurapika's Hunter License. The ticket verifier leaves and goes to make a phone call.

In the past, Kurapika's Master brought him some fish for dinner and tried to convince Kurapika that his quest for vengeance won't help him. The next day, Kurapika struggles to conjure chains and physically strains himself so much that he falls to his knees. Later, while attempting to conjure chains again, Kurapika's eyes go scarlet, and in his visions, he sinks to the bottom of the lake. The next day, he bites open his finger. Watching his blood drain from his hand helps him to better picture chains coming out of him; he can conjure many chains that sink into the lake, but in the process, Kurapika loses so much blood that he passes out in the lake and sinks. Kurapika's Master comes to save him, carrying him out of the water.

That night, Kurapika's Master tries to reason with Kurapika that he is pushing himself too far, but Kurapika insists that his Master doesn't understand. To aid Kurapika, his Master informs Kurapika that he may be able to create something close to an unbreakable chain, if that's what why wants, by making a contract with conditions. Kurapika decides to wager his life as part of the conditions.

Back on the train, Melody can hear the train stopping and six men boarding, looking for someone. The ticket verifier informs them of Kurapika's location, and they crowd outside Kurapika's train car door, waiting to strike. Melody informs Kurapika of the men and some details about them and says they are likely there to steal his Hunter License. Kurapika thanks Melody for the information and opens the car door before swiftly disarming the men, who run off after seeing that they are out-matched.

In another flashback, Kurapika arrives at the Sengi Guild again, looking for work, and the agent there is surprised at how quickly Kurapika was able to master Nen. He asks about job opportunities with an employer about the Yorknew City auction, and the agent finds him three options, allowing him to choose. 

Kurapika exits the train with Melody, and the two part on amicable terms. Kurapika is determined to fight alone, but Melody is certain they will meet again one day.
